source_jobs.title: Product Lead, Explore
source_jobs.description:
Stellic's Explore product is our newest and most open frontier. It's focused on the transfer experience — giving prospective and transfer students the guidance and pathways they need, and giving universities the tools to manage transfer rules, articulation, and admissions workflows. The roadmap is wide open. We're looking for a Product Lead who thrives in that kind of blank canvas, and who has the instincts to figure out what to build before anyone tells them.




ABOUT US

We're building a future where every student can move through their education with clarity, confidence, and purpose. Our platform transforms the student experience, giving them the tools they need to make the right decisions about their degree plans, track their journey, and build an education and career path that's truly their own.

Stellic is a fast-growing startup backed by leading social impact investors, partnering with top institutions like Cornell, Columbia, UVA, and Ohio State to support over 1 million students across 7 countries. As we scale to serve more universities worldwide, we remain deeply committed to this mission, driven by our values, and committed to building a high-performance team that's here to make a lasting difference on both students' lives and higher education.




ABOUT YOU

You've built products from scratch before — not contributed to someone else's vision, but started with a problem and figured out what to build. You're energized by ambiguity rather than paralyzed by it, and you know how to balance deep partner discovery with the conviction to make calls before all the data is in. You move fast, you learn constantly, and you hold yourself accountable for the outcome, not just the output.




AS OUR PRODUCT LEAD FOR EXPLORE, YOU WILL OWN

 - The Explore product, end to end: Explore is Stellic's transfer experience product — covering transfer pathways, rules management, articulation, prospective student profiles, and admissions workflows. You're the ultimate owner of it, which right now means defining what it becomes.

 - The roadmap from the ground up: There's no inherited backlog to manage here. You'll do the discovery, identify the highest-leverage problems, and build a roadmap that earns trust with both university partners and internal leadership.

 - Partner-driven discovery: You'll be in the room — on calls, on-site when needed — learning how transfer processes actually work at different institutions and turning that into product decisions that scale.

 - Engineering partnership: You'll work daily with our engineering teams in the US and Pakistan, thinking through architecture, trade-offs, and sequencing together from the earliest stages of the product.

 - Pattern recognition across institutions: Transfer rules and requirements vary enormously across universities. You'll find the common threads and design solutions that work across many schools without building custom one-offs.

 - The long game: Even in a greenfield product, every decision you make shapes what's possible later. You'll need to hold the immediate build and the long-term platform vision at the same time.




TO BE SUCCESSFUL, YOU SHOULD HAVE

 - 6+ years of PM experience with demonstrable growth in scope and ownership over time

 - True 0→1 experience — you've defined a product problem from nothing, built conviction around a solution, and shipped something real

 - Comfort with ambiguity — you don't need a clear spec to get started; you know how to structure discovery, form hypotheses, and make directional calls with incomplete information

 - Strong partner instincts — you're at home in customer conversations, you ask better questions than most, and you translate what you hear into product decisions rather than feature lists

 - Technical depth without needing to write code — comfortable in architecture discussions and able to think through how decisions made today constrain or enable what's possible tomorrow

 - An ownership mindset — on an early-stage prod

source_jobs.title: Product Support Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Product Support Engineering is the layer between our partners and our product — where partner trust gets earned in real time, and where every ticket is both an outcome to deliver and a signal worth acting on. The role isn't to move tickets faster; it's to diagnose deeply, kill recurring issues at the source, and make sure the product gets better because of what the queue teaches us. We're looking for a Product Support Engineer who wants to own that bar — and who thinks like an owner, not a ticket-closer.

 


ABOUT US

We’re building a future where every student can move through their education with clarity, confidence, and purpose. Our platform transforms the student experience, giving them then tools they need to make the right decisions about their degree plans, track their journey, and build an education and career path that’s truly their own.

Stellic is a fast-growing startup backed by leading social impact investors, partnering with top institutions like Cornell, Columbia, MIT, and Princeton to support over 1 million students across 7 countries. As we scale to serve more universities worldwide, we remain deeply committed to this mission, driven by our values, and committed to building a high-performance team that’s here to make a lasting difference on both students’ lives and higher education.

 


ABOUT YOU

You've spent a year or two working close to customers and close to the system — in a support engineering, technical support, or solutions role at a SaaS company. You read logs fluently, open devtools before opening a ticket, and push past "I don't know" until you find the cause. When an issue genuinely needs an engineer, you write the kind of escalation they can act on, quickly; when it shouldn't need one twice, you write the doc, build the macro, or script the fix to make sure it doesn't come back. You take ownership of the outcome, not just the activity, and you're ready to do it on a product that genuinely matters to the people using it.

 


AS OUR PRODUCT SUPPORT ENGINEER, YOU WILL OWN

About half the week is queue work; the other half is the process and tooling work that makes future queues smaller — better KB content, sharper diagnostic tooling, structural fixes that prevent recurrence. Across both, you'll own:

 - Queue health, with the bar high. You hold the line on SLA, CSAT, and ticket hygiene across every incoming ticket. Your queue data is always clean enough to inform real product decisions.

 - Diagnostic depth across your product areas. You'll own a defined set of product areas end-to-end including reading logs, querying data, reproducing issues, and forming hypotheses that hold up. You resolve the majority of Tier 2 escalations before they reach engineering.

 - Engineering handoffs engineers actually trust. The escalations you write resolve in one round-trip — the engineer has enough context to act without back-and-forth.

 - Killing recurring issues at the source. When an issue shows up twice, you partner with Product and use the right tools, like knowledge content or AI deflection, to make sure it does not show up a third time.

 - The knowledge and tooling layer. You leave KB articles, AI deflection content, support guides, the macro library better than you found it. You also contribute to the diagnostic tools the team builds for the queue.

 - Partnership with the AI tier. First-touch deflection runs through AI. You'll work alongside it, refining its content, training it on what humans solve, and lifting deflection in your areas without dropping CSAT.

 


TO BE SUCCESSFUL, YOU SHOULD HAVE

 - At least 1 to 2 years in technical support, customer engineering, or solutions engineering at a B2B SaaS company, ideally somewhere with real complexity (data accuracy, integrations, or environments where being wrong has consequences). That's a floor, not a ceiling: if you're further along in your career and the role still excites you, we want to hear from you.

 - A Bachelor's in Computer Science, Softw

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Stellic is growing, and we're hiring Senior Software Engineers across all of our product teams. If you're a great engineer who cares about impact and wants to own meaningful work — we want to hear from you. Our HQ is in San Mateo and we have a hybrid office culture so we would prefer that you could come into the office too but we are also open to great remote (within the US) candidates.




ABOUT US

We're building a future where every student can move through their education with clarity, confidence, and purpose. Our platform transforms the student experience, giving them the tools they need to make the right decisions about their degree plans, track their journey, and build an education and career path that's truly their own.

Stellic is a fast-growing startup backed by leading social impact investors, partnering with top institutions like Cornell, Columbia, UVA, and Ohio State to support over 1 million students across 7 countries. As we scale to serve more universities worldwide, we remain deeply committed to this mission, driven by our values, and committed to building a high-performance team that's here to make a lasting difference on both students' lives and higher education.




STELLIC ENGINEERS

Stellic engineers combine a rare blend of curiosity, creativity, and business acumen with the ability to deliver pragmatic solutions at high velocity with uncompromising quality. Our mission is to help students, and our engineers always have that in mind as they are building.




ABOUT YOU

You're a strong engineer who takes real ownership of your work — from the first line of code to production. You care about quality, move with urgency, and get energized by problems that have genuine impact. You thrive in small, high-trust teams where you're expected to think, not just execute.




AS OUR SENIOR SOFTWARE ENGINEER, YOU WILL OWN

 - Feature delivery end-to-end: Lead impactful projects from scoping through release — shipping new capabilities and improvements that directly change how students and advisors experience Stellic.

 - Code quality and standards: Hold the bar high on testing, security, accessibility, and the practices that keep a codebase healthy as it scales.

 - Cross-functional collaboration: Partner closely with Product and Design to make sure what gets built actually solves the right problems — and stay close to customer outcomes.

 - Mentorship: Help junior engineers grow by sharing context, giving direct feedback, and modeling strong engineering habits.

 - Engineering initiatives: Contribute to broader efforts that improve scalability, operational efficiency, and how the team ships.




TO BE SUCCESSFUL, YOU SHOULD HAVE

 - 5+ years of experience in software engineering

 - Strong fundamentals across front-end and/or back-end development — we care more about how you think and build than the specific stack

 - Experience shipping production software in a collaborative, fast-moving environment

 - Testing and CI/CD experience — you know what good looks like and hold yourself to it

 - Strong communication skills and comfort working cross-functionally with Product, Design, and fellow engineers

 - A track record of ownership — you've taken projects from idea to outcome and felt accountable for the result




OUR ENGINEERING TEAMS

We're hiring across Platform, Progress, Integration, Explore, and Care.

 - Platform: Owns critical systems such as search, which underpins much of the user experience, as well as a growing set of AI-driven capabilities, including AI Analytics and the AI platform. These systems span both end-user-facing features and internal building blocks used across the product.

 - Plan: Owns the systems that enable students to plan their academic journey, including degree planning & pathways, scheduling, and registration.

 - Progress: Owns Stellic's Degree Progress product, which enables students and administrators to track progress toward degree requirements and academic goals.

 - Integration: Re

source_jobs.title: Tech Lead, Progress
source_jobs.description:
Stellic is growing! We're looking for a Progress Lead to own the technical direction of our Degree Progress product. We're evolving from a search-based system to a SAT solver–driven architecture, and we need a technical leader who can operationalize that algorithmic power into a product that students and universities can genuinely trust.

 


ABOUT US

We're building a future where every student can move through their education with clarity, confidence, and purpose. Our platform transforms the student experience, giving them the tools they need to make the right decisions about their degree plans, track their journey, and build an education and career path that's truly their own.

Stellic is a fast-growing startup backed by leading social impact investors, partnering with top institutions like Cornell, Columbia, UVA, and Ohio State to support over 1 million students across 7 countries. As we scale to serve more universities worldwide, we remain deeply committed to this mission, driven by our values, and committed to building a high-performance team that's here to make a lasting difference on both students' lives and higher education.

 


STELLIC ENGINEERS

Stellic engineers combine a rare blend of curiosity, creativity, and business acumen with the ability to deliver pragmatic solutions at high velocity with uncompromising quality. Our mission is to help students, and our engineers always have that in mind as they are building.




THE PROGRESS TEAM

The Progress team owns Stellic's Degree Progress https://www.stellic.com/progress product, which enables students and administrators to track progress toward degree requirements and academic goals. The team is split across two closely related areas:

 - Compute: focuses on the underlying algorithm and correctness of results

 - Product: focuses on applying, validating, and presenting those results in a reliable and intuitive way

This role leads the entire team, working in close partnership with our algorithms lead. The team's primary focus right now is improving the scalability and reliability of the system, with a heavy emphasis on building purpose-built testing frameworks to validate complex and highly variable degree requirements.

 


ABOUT YOU

You've built systems where correctness isn't negotiable — where the output needs to be right, explainable, and testable across a massive range of edge cases. You think deeply about validation, debugging, and what it means to deliver reliable outcomes in complex domains. You're as comfortable partnering with an algorithms lead as you are setting technical direction for your own team.

 


AS OUR PROGRESS LEAD, YOU WILL OWN

 - Degree Progress systems, end to end: Own the systems that orchestrate and operationalize results from the SAT solver into user-facing experiences — applying, validating, and presenting degree progress outcomes in a way that students and advisors can trust.

 - Algorithm-to-product integration: Partner closely with our algorithms lead to ensure seamless integration between compute outputs and product behavior across the full range of edge cases that complex degree requirements introduce.

 - Purpose-built testing frameworks: Design and implement testing infrastructure specifically built to validate complex and highly variable degree requirements — one of the team's biggest current investments.

 - Correctness and reliability: Ensure the system delivers reliable, explainable outcomes across edge cases, with clear guarantees around performance and behavior.

 - Technical prioritization: Drive alignment on engineering-driven priorities alongside product priorities.

 - Engineering excellence: Drive engineering excellence by defining and mentoring on best practices, design patterns, and code quality.

 


TO BE SUCCESSFUL, YOU SHOULD HAVE

 - 5+ years of backend engineering experience operating at scale, with a track record of leading complex implementations as a technical lead

 - Strong intuition for designing syst

source_jobs.title: Tech Lead, Explore
source_jobs.description:
Stellic is growing! We're looking for a Tech Lead our ****newest product, Explore. Explore is a platform that helps prospective and transfer students plan their academic pathways before they ever set foot on campus. This is a 0→1 product area with meaningful architectural stakes, and we are looking for a technical leader who thrives in ambiguity and can build for the long term while moving fast.




ABOUT US

We're building a future where every student can move through their education with clarity, confidence, and purpose. Our platform transforms the student experience, giving them the tools they need to make the right decisions about their degree plans, track their journey, and build an education and career path that's truly their own.

Stellic is a fast-growing startup backed by leading social impact investors, partnering with top institutions like Cornell, Columbia, UVA, and Ohio State to support over 1 million students across 7 countries. As we scale to serve more universities worldwide, we remain deeply committed to this mission, driven by our values, and committed to building a high-performance team that's here to make a lasting difference on both students' lives and higher education.




STELLIC ENGINEERS

Stellic engineers combine a rare blend of curiosity, creativity, and business acumen with the ability to deliver pragmatic solutions at high velocity with uncompromising quality. Our mission is to help students, and our engineers always have that in mind as they are building.




THE EXPLORE TEAM

The Explore team owns Stellic's newest product, focused on helping prospective and transfer students plan their academic pathways before enrolling at an institution. This product extends Stellic beyond enrolled students, introducing new workflows and user journeys centered around discovery, planning, and decision-making prior to joining a university.

Explore is also the first system at Stellic to introduce multi-tenant concepts, which means the team is breaking new ground in how data is shared, isolated, and managed across institutions.

As a newer product area, the team operates with a higher degree of ambiguity and iteration — balancing rapid product development with the need to establish a strong architectural foundation that can scale.

As technical lead on Explore, you'll play a key role in shaping both the product and the underlying systems, helping define how Stellic expands into new user segments and new architectural paradigms.




ABOUT YOU

You've built product surfaces from 0 to 1 before, and you know how to make smart architectural calls when the path forward isn't fully defined yet. You're comfortable operating with ambiguity, and you bring strong judgment to decisions that will have long-term consequences. You understand that speed and quality aren't opposites — you know how to move fast while laying foundations that hold.




AS OUR EXPLORE LEAD, YOU WILL OWN

 - The Explore product, end to end: Own the technical direction and evolution of the Explore product, from new capabilities for prospective and transfer students to the underlying systems that power them.

 - Multi-tenant architecture foundations: Establish the foundational patterns for Stellic's first multi-tenant system — defining how data is shared, isolated, and managed across institutions in a way that can scale.

 - Pragmatic architectural decisions in an evolving product space: Make clear, defensible calls in a fast-moving environment, balancing the speed of iteration with the need to build systems that hold up over time.

 - Clean, ergonomic APIs and reliable abstractions: Build and maintain the APIs and abstractions that handle complex business logic and data flows, keeping things simple for the engineers who build on top of them.

 - Engineering excellence: Drive engineering excellence by defining and mentoring on best practices, design patterns, and code quality across the team.




TO BE SUCCESSFUL, YOU SHOULD HAVE

 - 5+ years of backend o

source_jobs.title: Marketing Specialist, Product & Content
source_jobs.description:
Stellic is growing! We're looking for a Marketing Specialist, Product & Content to join our Marketing team at the intersection of product marketing and content. This is a hands-on, high-ownership role where you'll shape how Stellic is positioned, build the content and campaigns that drive awareness and pipeline, and create the assets that help our team win in market.




ABOUT US

We're building a future where every student can move through their education with clarity, confidence, and purpose. Our platform transforms the student experience, giving them then tools they need to make the right decisions about their degree plans, track their journey, and build an education and career path that's truly their own.

Stellic is a fast-growing startup backed by leading social impact investors, partnering with top institutions like Cornell, Columbia, UVA, and Ohio State to support over 1 million students across 7 countries. As we scale to serve more universities worldwide, we remain deeply committed to this mission, driven by our values, and committed to building a high-performance team that's here to make a lasting difference on both students' lives and higher education.




ABOUT YOU

You're a strong writer and content creator who doesn't want to just write — you want to own the full picture. You think in positioning and messaging frameworks, not just blog posts, and you're energized by the intersection of product and go-to-market. You're comfortable in front of a camera, fluent in short-form and social content, and excited about using AI tools to move faster and smarter. You're the kind of person who sees a content gap or a positioning opportunity and fills it before anyone asks.




AS OUR MARKETING SPECIALIST, YOU WILL OWN

 - Content Creation: Produce a high volume of compelling content across formats — written, video, short-form social, and more — that speaks directly to the challenges facing higher education institutions and the value Stellic delivers.

 - Product Marketing Support: Support development of key PMM deliverables including messaging frameworks, positioning docs, and launch materials — translating what Stellic builds into narratives that resonate with buyers and champions.

 - Campaign Messaging: Develop messaging and content for demand generation campaigns in close partnership with the broader marketing team, ensuring every asset is sharp, on-brand, and built to convert.

 - Sales Enablement: Build and maintain the assets that help our sales team sell — battle cards, one-pagers, and pitch materials that reflect sharp, up-to-date positioning.

 - SEO/AEO Content: Create and optimize content for search and answer engine discoverability, helping Stellic show up where buyers are already looking.

 - AI-Powered Workflows: Leverage AI tools and platforms to build bots, agents, and automations that help the marketing team move faster and punch above its weight.
   
   


TO BE SUCCESSFUL, YOU SHOULD HAVE

 - 2–4 years of experience in product marketing, content marketing, or a related role at a B2B SaaS company

 - Exceptional writing skills — you can write clearly and persuasively across formats, audiences, and channels

 - Video content experience — you're comfortable creating and editing short-form video for social and marketing purposes

 - Hands-on experience with AI tools — you actively use and experiment with AI to build workflows, automate tasks, and create content faster

 - Familiarity with SEO/AEO best practices and how to write content that ranks and answers

 - Strong research instincts — you can synthesize competitive intel and market trends into clear, actionable insights

 - A bias toward execution — you don't wait for perfect briefs, you figure it out and ship it

 - Bachelor's degree in Marketing, Communications, or a related field
   
   


WHY YOU'LL LOVE WORKING AT STELLIC

 - Shape the future of higher education at a company leading the transformation of student success.

 - Work alongside an ambitio

source_jobs.title: Integration Lead
source_jobs.description:
Stellic is growing! We're looking for an Integration Lead to own the systems that connect university Student Information Systems to Stellic. This is mission-critical infrastructure where data correctness and reliability directly affect everything our product does — and we need a technical leader to drive our integration architecture into its next phase.

 


ABOUT US

We're building a future where every student can move through their education with clarity, confidence, and purpose. Our platform transforms the student experience, giving them the tools they need to make the right decisions about their degree plans, track their journey, and build an education and career path that's truly their own.

Stellic is a fast-growing startup backed by leading social impact investors, partnering with top institutions like Cornell, Columbia, UVA, and Ohio State to support over 1 million students across 7 countries. As we scale to serve more universities worldwide, we remain deeply committed to this mission, driven by our values, and committed to building a high-performance team that's here to make a lasting difference on both students' lives and higher education.

 


STELLIC ENGINEERS

Stellic engineers combine a rare blend of curiosity, creativity, and business acumen with the ability to deliver pragmatic solutions at high velocity with uncompromising quality. Our mission is to help students, and our engineers always have that in mind as they are building.

 


THE INTEGRATION TEAM

The Integration team is responsible for the critical bridge between university Student Information Systems (SIS) and Stellic. This integration layer is foundational to ensuring that Stellic's data remains accurate, up-to-date, and trustworthy. The team owns the systems that power this bridge end-to-end, including integration pipelines, data models, and the interfaces that expose external data across the product. This requires navigating a wide range of external systems, each with its own complexity and nuances, and translating that into standardized, coherent internal representations that the rest of the product can rely on.

The team is also responsible for evolving Stellic's integration approach, consolidating multiple legacy patterns into a new, well-defined standard that will support future scale and product development.

As a technical lead on Integration, you will play a central role in shaping how external data flows through Stellic, with direct impact on the reliability and trustworthiness of the entire platform.




ABOUT YOU

You've designed and operated high-reliability integration systems before, and you know what it takes to build for data correctness at scale — fault-tolerant, observable, and easy to debug when something goes wrong. You bring strong judgment to defining standards and patterns, and you know how to make complex, messy external systems fit cleanly into a coherent internal model.

 


AS OUR INTEGRATION LEAD, YOU WILL OWN

 - SIS integration systems, end to end: Own the technical direction and evolution of the systems that bridge university Student Information Systems and Stellic — including integration pipelines, data models, and the interfaces that expose external data across the product.

 - A new integration standard: Define and drive the adoption of a consolidated integration architecture, replacing multiple legacy patterns with a consistent, scalable approach that will support Stellic's next phase of growth.

 - Data correctness and reliability: Ensure systems are fault-tolerant, observable, and easy to debug, with clear guarantees around data integrity and behavior across a wide range of heterogeneous external data sources.

 - External system complexity: Design systems that reliably handle varied SIS environments while maintaining clean, well-defined internal representations the rest of the product can depend on.

 - Engineering excellence: Drive engineering excellence by defining and mentoring on best practices, design patte

source_jobs.title: Product Lead, Progress
source_jobs.description:
The Progress product is the foundation everything else at Stellic is built on. Every student plan, every advisor conversation, every graduation clearance starts with an accurate representation of where a student stands against their degree requirements. We're looking for a Product Lead who wants to own that foundation — and who thinks like a lead, not a contributor.




ABOUT US

We're building a future where every student can move through their education with clarity, confidence, and purpose. Our platform transforms the student experience, giving them the tools they need to make the right decisions about their degree plans, track their journey, and build an education and career path that's truly their own.

Stellic is a fast-growing startup backed by leading social impact investors, partnering with top institutions like Cornell, Columbia, UVA, and Ohio State to support over 1 million students across 7 countries. As we scale to serve more universities worldwide, we remain deeply committed to this mission, driven by our values, and committed to building a high-performance team that's here to make a lasting difference on both students' lives and higher education.




ABOUT YOU

You've built complex products before — the kind where accuracy isn't a nice-to-have, it's the whole game. You think like an owner: you don't wait to be told what to build, you figure out what matters and make it happen. You go deep with engineers rather than around them, and you hold yourself accountable for outcomes, not output. You're ready to be the own the customer impact of a product that genuinely matters.




AS OUR PRODUCT LEAD FOR PROGRESS, YOU WILL OWN

 - The Progress product, end to end: You're the ultimate owner of one of Stellic's core products — responsible for how universities define academic requirements and how we evaluate every student's progress against them. Everything downstream depends on getting this right.

 - Problem decomposition: You'll take long-horizon, ambiguous challenges and break them into concrete, iterative steps that deliver real value while moving toward the bigger picture.

 - Engineering partnership: You'll work daily with our engineering teams in the US and Pakistan — not handing over specs, but thinking through trade-offs, system design, problem solving and architecture decisions together.

 - University partner relationships: You'll join discovery calls, build trust with academic institutions, and translate what you hear into decisions that scale across many schools, not just one.

 - Pattern recognition at scale: You'll identify what's common across universities — even when they describe their needs differently — and design solutions that serve many without building one-offs.

 - The long game for Progress: Every feature you ship should connect to where this product is going. You'll hold both the immediate problem and the long-term vision at the same time, and make the call when they're in tension.




TO BE SUCCESSFUL, YOU SHOULD HAVE

 - 8+ years of PM experience with clear growth in scope and ownership over time

 - Genuine 0→1 ownership — you were the person who defined the problem, made the key calls, and was accountable for the outcome

 - Experience in complex, accuracy-critical enterprise B2B — fintech compliance, data governance, healthcare systems, legal tech, or similar domains where being wrong has real consequences
   
   - Technical depth without needing to write code — comfortable in architecture discussions, able to reason through trade-offs, and capable of going deep on a problem you haven't seen before and think through solutions with engineers vs just handing off problems

 - Strong discovery instincts — you talk directly to customers, push past stated requirements to find the underlying need, and use data to validate what you're hearing

 - An ownership mindset — you feel the weight of the outcome and act accordingly

 - Clear, engaging communication — you can make a complex decision feel obv

source_jobs.title: SENIOR PRODUCT DESIGNER
source_jobs.description:
JOB DESCRIPTION

Design and develop user interfaces and digital product flows, including wireframes, mock-ups, high-fidelity prototypes, and final UI specifications for web and mobile applications. Lead the entire product design lifecycle: gather requirements, conduct user research and usability testing, synthesize user feedback and analytics, iterate on design, and deliver production-ready designs. Collaborate closely with product managers, engineers, researchers, and business stakeholders to define product requirements, design strategy, and roadmap. Maintain and evolve a design system to ensure consistent, accessible, and scalable UI across multiple products and platforms. Provide design leadership and mentorship: review design work of junior designers, provide feedback, and ensure adherence to best practices in UX, interaction design, information architecture, and accessibility. Translate business needs, technical constraints, and user needs into intuitive, user-centered design solutions. Work with engineering and development teams to ensure feasibility and accurate implementation of designs, and participate in design QA as needed.



REQUIREMENTS

Education: Bachelor’s degree in Interaction Design, Human-Computer Interaction, Graphic Design, Visual Design or related field.

Experience: 3 years of experience as a Product Designer, Product Designer Intern, Graphic & Web Designer or closely related position.

Special Skills: 3 years experience with each of the following: user experience (UX) and user interface (UI) design; designing for both web and mobile platforms; collaborating with cross-functional teams (Product Management, Engineering, QA); using analytics and behavioral-insight tools; designing responsive web applications; and modern design tools such as Figma, Sketch and Adobe Suite. The position also requires knowledge of web content accessibility guidelines (WCAG)



WORK LOCATION

400 S. EL CAMINO REAL, SUITE 650, SAN MATEO, CA 94402

The position offers the benefit of remote work up to 2 days per week.

Travel to company events, 2-3 times a year, for 4-5 days each trip.



SALARY

$140,000 to $ 180,000 Per Year




STELLIC IS AN EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER

Our company values diversity and believes diverse teams make innovation possible. We encourage all qualified applicants from any race, color, religion, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, national origin, disability status, protected veteran status, or other characteristics to apply.

source_jobs.title: Business Development Representative
source_jobs.description:
Stellic is growing. We're looking for a Business Development Representative who understands higher education and wants to do outbound that actually works — research-driven, human, and worth someone's time to respond to. This is a quota-carrying role where success is measured by meeting quality and pipeline generated.




ABOUT US

Stellic partners with universities and colleges to help students see a clear path to graduation and actually follow it. Our degree planning platform is used at institutions across the U.S., Canada, and Australia — including Cornell, Columbia, UVA, and Ohio State — supporting over 1 million students across 7 countries.

We're a small team doing work that matters. We're backed by leading social impact investors and we're growing.




ABOUT YOU

You don't have to come from a traditional sales background. Some of our best fits have come out of higher education entirely. What matters is that you understand the space, you take goals seriously, and you can write and speak effectively.

You get why registrars, VPs of Student Success, and provosts operate the way they do. You know they're dealing with shrinking budgets, enrollment pressure, and stretched teams — and you lead with something worth their time, not a pitch. You're curious, coachable, and comfortable working in a role where your output is visible.




WHAT YOU'LL OWN

 - Pipeline Generation: Own a monthly pipeline target through strategic, personalized outbound outreach and inbound follow-up to higher education institutions.

 - Institutional Research: Research schools and people before you reach out. Understand what's actually happening there and craft messages that reflect it.

 - Meeting Targets: Set 5–8 quality meetings monthly and maintain a strong pipeline conversion rate.

 - Conference Presence: Attend 2–3 regional and national conferences annually to represent Stellic and build relationships face-to-face.

 - AE Collaboration: Partner with Account Executives on territory strategy, account priorities, and handoffs — and keep iterating on what's working.

 - Tool Fluency: Use AI and sales tools (Claude, Gong Engage, and a growing stack) to work faster and know when human judgment matters more than automation.




TO BE SUCCESSFUL, YOU SHOULD HAVE

 - Some real connection to higher education — you've worked at a university, studied student affairs, worked in admissions or advising, or sold into institutions OR 1–3 years in sales, business development, or a related field, with a genuine interest in the higher ed space

 - Strong writing: short, specific, no filler

 - A track record of setting goals and following through

 - Comfort on the phone without being dependent on it — you know when a call is the right move

 - Curiosity about how AI is changing prospecting and a willingness to experiment

 - Familiarity with CRMs and modern sales tools

 - Ability to get to San Mateo 2–3 days a week

Bonus: familiarity with student information systems or edtech.




WHY STELLIC

 - Be part of a mission-driven company making a real difference for students

 - Learn deeply about higher education and enterprise SaaS sales

 - Work closely with a supportive, high-performing team that values growth and ownership

 - Competitive compensation, equity, and benefits

 - Flexible, outcomes-focused work environment

 - Comprehensive health coverage, 401k, commuter benefits, and team retreats




STELLIC IS AN EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER

Our company values diversity and believes diverse teams make innovation possible. We encourage all qualified applicants from any race, color, religion, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, national origin, disability status, protected veteran status, or other characteristics to apply.

source_jobs.title: Initiative Delivery Lead
source_jobs.description:
Stellic is growing, and we're looking for an Initiative Delivery Lead to own end-to-end delivery of strategic initiatives across a portfolio of university partners. This isn't project coordination — it's program ownership. You're the person who looks across everything a partner has in motion, understands how each piece connects, and makes sure it all lands.




ABOUT US

We're building a future where every student can move through their education with clarity, confidence, and purpose. Our platform transforms the student experience, giving them the tools they need to make the right decisions about their degree plans, track their journey, and build an education and career path that's truly their own.

Stellic is a fast-growing startup backed by leading social impact investors, partnering with top institutions like Cornell, Columbia, UVA, and Ohio State to support over 1 million students across 7 countries. As we scale to serve more universities worldwide, we remain deeply committed to this mission, driven by our values, and committed to building a high-performance team that's here to make a lasting difference on both students' lives and higher education.




ABOUT YOU

You don't wait for clarity — you create it. A partner walks in with a goal like "we want better student outcomes" and you walk out with a scoped initiative, defined success metrics, clear owners, and a finish line everyone agrees on. You hold delivery together across competing priorities and multiple concurrent workstreams, and when one thing shifts, you immediately map the downstream impact on everything else in flight. You think at the program level, not just the project level. You care more about what a project is actually accomplishing than about checking it off a list — and that comes through in how you show up for partners, how you build relationships, and how you handle the hard conversations. You hold yourself and others to their commitments without damaging the relationships that make the work possible.




AS OUR INITIATIVE DELIVERY LEAD, YOU WILL OWN

 - Initiative Planning: Turn partner goals into concrete, actionable initiatives with defined scope, sequenced milestones, owners, dependencies, risks, resources, and exit criteria. Make "done" unambiguous before the work begins rather than after.

 - Program-Level Execution: Manage a portfolio of simultaneous partner implementations, tracking overall program health across workstreams — not just individual project status. When something shifts in one area, you understand the ripple effects and respond at the program level. Delivery outcomes are always front and center: initiatives on time, on scope, high partner confidence, and a delivery NPS that reflects the quality of the work.

 - Platform Fluency: Build genuine knowledge of the Stellic platform and how our partners are using it — not just what it does, but why it matters. That fluency is what lets you stay credible in implementation conversations and be a real partner in the room.

 - Partner Communications: Own project health and partner communications, both internal and partner-facing, in close partnership with the Partner Strategy Lead. Keep everyone informed and confident at every level, from campus project managers to exec sponsors. No one should ever have to ask where things stand.

 - Cross-Functional Coordination: Work closely with Product Delivery Leads and bring the right internal stakeholders into the conversation at the right time. Reduce escalations by building context ahead of problems and move things forward through influence and trust.

 - Risk, Resourcing, and Accountability: Proactively manage risk and resourcing. Know when to escalate, how to prioritize, and what tradeoffs make the most sense. Hold partners and internal teams accountable to their commitments, and handle those conversations with care.

 - Partner Advocacy: Keep the partner's voice at the center and advocate for what they need across the organization.




TO BE SUCCES

source_jobs.title: Lead Data Infrastructure Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Stellic is growing! We're looking for a Lead Data Engineer to own the next phase of our data platform that will power everything from internal decision-making to customer-facing insights — and we need a technical leader who can evolve it to support increasing scale, complexity, and product integration.




ABOUT US

We're building a future where every student can move through their education with clarity, confidence, and purpose. Our platform transforms the student experience, giving them the tools they need to make the right decisions about their degree plans, track their journey, and build an education and career path that's truly their own.

Stellic is a fast-growing startup backed by leading social impact investors, partnering with top institutions like Cornell, Columbia, UVA, and Ohio State to support over 1 million students across 7 countries. As we scale to serve more universities worldwide, we remain deeply committed to this mission, driven by our values, and committed to building a high-performance team that's here to make a lasting difference on both students' lives and higher education.




STELLIC ENGINEERS

Stellic engineers combine a rare blend of curiosity, creativity, and business acumen with the ability to deliver pragmatic solutions at high velocity with uncompromising quality. Our mission is to help students, and our engineers always have that in mind as they are building.




THE DATA TEAM

Stellic's data platform will be critical part of how we operate and deliver value, and will power everything from internal decision-making to customer-facing insights. The platform spans data ingestion, transformation, aggregation, and presentation — with much of the foundation in place, but requiring significant iteration and expansion to support new use cases and growing scale.

We work with data that is highly variable across partners and regions, requiring thoughtful system design to balance standardization with flexibility. In addition to the internal platform, this role is responsible for designing, building, and maintaining customer-facing data systems that extend the core architecture directly to university partners.

As a technical lead on Data, you will own the next phase of our data architecture, shaping both the platform and how data is leveraged across the product. This is a hands-on, high-impact role focused on building and operating a data platform that is scalable, adaptable, and aligned with both internal and product needs. This role will play a central part in shaping both the platform and how data is leveraged across the product.




ABOUT YOU

You've designed and operated data pipelines at scale before, and you understand what it means to build systems with clear guarantees around correctness and consistency. You think carefully about data models, lineage, and validation — and you're equally comfortable owning the internal platform and the customer-facing products built on top of it.




AS OUR LEAD DATA ENGINEER, YOU WILL OWN

 - Data platform, end to end: Own the design, evolution, and operation of Stellic's data pipelines from ingestion through transformation, aggregation, and presentation.

 - Customer-facing data systems: Design, build, and maintain customer-facing data products built on top of the core data architecture, extending the platform's value directly to university partners.

 - Scalable, reliable data processing: Build and maintain systems that ensure consistent and reliable data processing at scale, with clear guarantees around correctness and behavior.

 - Data models across varied requirements: Design data models and pipelines that balance standardization with flexibility to support diverse regional and institutional requirements.

 - Compliance and partner requirements: Ensure systems meet regional and partner-specific data requirements and compliance constraints.

 - Platform evolution: Identify and drive improvements across the data platform to support new use cases and increased

source_jobs.title: Tech Lead, Plan
source_jobs.description:
Stellic is growing! We're looking for a ****Tech Lead for the Plan team that owns the systems that power how students plan their academic journeys — from degree planning and pathways to scheduling and registration. This is a high-stakes, high-visibility area of our product, and we need a technical leader who can keep these systems reliable under peak load while shaping the next generation of planning experiences.




ABOUT US

We're building a future where every student can move through their education with clarity, confidence, and purpose. Our platform transforms the student experience, giving them the tools they need to make the right decisions about their degree plans, track their journey, and build an education and career path that's truly their own.

Stellic is a fast-growing startup backed by leading social impact investors, partnering with top institutions like Cornell, Columbia, UVA, and Ohio State to support over 1 million students across 7 countries. As we scale to serve more universities worldwide, we remain deeply committed to this mission, driven by our values, and committed to building a high-performance team that's here to make a lasting difference on both students' lives and higher education.




STELLIC ENGINEERS

Stellic engineers combine a rare blend of curiosity, creativity, and business acumen with the ability to deliver pragmatic solutions at high velocity with uncompromising quality. Our mission is to help students, and our engineers always have that in mind as they are building.




THE PLAN TEAM

The Plan team owns the systems that enable students to plan their academic journey — including degree planning and pathways, scheduling, and registration. This area is actively evolving from curriculum-centric planning toward more flexible, pathway- and career-oriented experiences, requiring the team to rethink how students explore options and make decisions about their academic paths.

A critical part of this system is registration, which allows students to enroll in courses at their university. Registration periods are high-stakes moments where reliability, performance, and seamless execution are non-negotiable.

The team is currently focused on improving the reliability, scalability, and performance of these systems, while continuing to evolve the product to support more dynamic and personalized planning experiences.

As technical lead on Plan, you'll play a key role in ensuring these systems operate predictably and efficiently under peak load, while shaping the next generation of planning and pathway experiences.




ABOUT YOU

You've built and operated reliable systems in high-stakes environments before, and you know what it takes to keep things running smoothly when the pressure is on. You have strong intuition for performance and concurrency, and you're equally comfortable thinking about long-term product evolution and near-term system stability. You care about getting the details right and can balance competing priorities without losing sight of either.




AS OUR PLAN LEAD, YOU WILL OWN

 - Planning, pathways, scheduling, and registration — end to end: Own the technical direction and evolution of the systems that power these core student-facing experiences across their academic journey.

 - Reliability of registration systems: Ensure registration systems perform predictably and efficiently during high-stakes registration periods, when students and institutions are counting on every interaction to work.

 - Systems built for peak load: Design systems that handle concurrency and peak demand gracefully, with clear strategies for testing and validating behavior at scale.

 - The shift to career-focused planning: Enable our product to evolve from degree-centric planning toward more flexible, pathway- and career-oriented experiences — without sacrificing the stability of the systems underneath.

 - Testing strategies at scale: Ensure quality by designing robust, scalable testing strategies that validate sys

source_jobs.title: Platform Lead
source_jobs.description:
Stellic is growing! We're looking for a Platform Lead to own the technical direction of the systems at the center of our architecture. We're investing heavily in AI capabilities, search infrastructure, and shared platform primitives — and we need a technical leader who can shape both what we build and how teams across engineering build on top of it.

 


ABOUT US

We're building a future where every student can move through their education with clarity, confidence, and purpose. Our platform transforms the student experience, giving them the tools they need to make the right decisions about their degree plans, track their journey, and build an education and career path that's truly their own.

Stellic is a fast-growing startup backed by leading social impact investors, partnering with top institutions like Cornell, Columbia, UVA, and Ohio State to support over 1 million students across 7 countries. As we scale to serve more universities worldwide, we remain deeply committed to this mission, driven by our values, and committed to building a high-performance team that's here to make a lasting difference on both students' lives and higher education.

 


STELLIC ENGINEERS

Stellic engineers combine a rare blend of curiosity, creativity, and business acumen with the ability to deliver pragmatic solutions at high velocity with uncompromising quality. Our mission is to help students, and our engineers always have that in mind as they are building.

 


THE PLATFORM TEAM

The Platform team is responsible for building both foundational infrastructure and key product surfaces that power Stellic's core experiences. The team owns critical systems such as search, which underpins much of the user experience, as well as a growing set of AI-driven capabilities, including AI Analytics and the AI platform. These systems span both end-user-facing features and internal building blocks used across the product.

In addition to owning features end-to-end, the team develops shared primitives such as workflow infrastructure and AI systems used broadly across engineering. The team is currently focused on improving the reliability and scalability of these systems, while continuing to build new capabilities that expand what's possible across the product.

As a technical lead on Platform, your work will have broad impact across teams and the overall product, shaping both the systems we build and how other teams build on top of them.

 


ABOUT YOU

You've built scalable AI platforms and ElasticSearch-powered search systems at scale, and you understand what it means to create infrastructure that other teams actually want to build on. You think carefully about abstractions and interfaces, hold a high bar for quality across shared systems, and bring genuine ownership to everything — from architecture decisions down to code review.

 


AS OUR PLATFORM LEAD, YOU WILL OWN

 - Search and AI platform: Own the technical direction and evolution of Stellic's search infrastructure and AI platform — from end-user features like search and AI Analytics to the internal systems teams use to build AI-powered capabilities across the product.

 - Shared platform primitives: Design and evolve shared building blocks — including workflow infrastructure and AI systems — used broadly across engineering. Your decisions shape how other teams build.

 - Quality of shared systems: Own consistency and quality across shared systems that have broad usage and impact across engineering. Establish clear patterns and abstractions that enable other teams to build on platform capabilities effectively.

 - Technical prioritization: Partner on prioritization of engineering-driven and product priorities, acting as a true peer to product leadership.

 - Engineering excellence: Drive engineering excellence by defining and mentoring on best practices, design patterns, and code quality across the team.

 


TO BE SUCCESSFUL, YOU SHOULD HAVE

 - 8+ years of backend engineering experience operating a

notes: Strong product focus, multiple tech lead roles, and a growing engineering team.
